title: Garden Bench
summary: A 2x3-framed outdoor bench with notched armrests and a slatted seat over a diagonal support beam — plain and sturdy rather than decorative, sized to fit exactly where you put it.
description:
The seat is supported by four short 2x2 blocks attached to the front and back aprons a half inch up from the bottom, plus a diagonal brace measured and cut to fit your own bench's actual diagonal rather than a fixed length — **measure your assembled frame before cutting this piece**, since small variations in board thickness change the diagonal enough to matter. Seven 2x3 slats screw down from underneath, spaced about 3/4" apart by eye.

The front and back legs attach to the apron assembly next, measured 20" up from the bottom of each leg to the underside of the seat, with three screws per leg — two into the seat, one into the apron. Bottom leg supports tie the four legs together lower down, predrilled through the front of each leg.

**The armrests are notched to the exact dimensions of your own 2x3 stock** — measure your actual boards before cutting the notches, since dimensional lumber varies enough to leave a gap or a too-tight fit otherwise. Predrill through the side of each armrest into the back leg, and screw down from the top into the front leg.

materials.name: Exterior primer and paint, or stain

cutList.note: Measure your own assembled bench diagonal before cutting — do not cut to this length blind.

cutList.part: Seat slats and bottom support beam
cutList.note: 7 used as seat slats, 1 as the bottom support beam.

cutList.part: Side aprons and bottom leg supports

cutList.note: Notched to the exact dimensions of your own 2x3 stock.

steps.title: Attach the seat supports
steps.body: Attach the 2x2 seat supports to the front and back aprons, half an inch up from the bottom, with the middle two supports 21" apart.

steps.title: Fit the diagonal support beam
steps.body: Measure the diagonal of your own assembled bench frame and cut the support beam to fit, mitering both ends to seat into the corners.

steps.title: Attach the seat slats
steps.body: Screw the seven seat slats down from underneath with 2" screws, spacing them about 3/4" apart.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Measure 20" up from the bottom of each leg for the underside of the seat, and attach with three 2" screws per leg — two into the seat, one into the apron.

steps.title: Add the bottom leg supports and beam
steps.body: Predrill through the front of each leg 10" up from the bottom and attach the bottom leg supports with two 3" screws per leg, then screw the bottom beam to the leg supports.

steps.title: Notch and attach the armrests
steps.body: Measure your own 2x3 stock and notch the armrests to fit exactly. Predrill through the side into the back leg and screw down from the top into the front leg.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply exterior primer and paint, or stain rated for outdoor use.
materials: Exterior primer and paint, or stain

unresolvedImages.alt: Outdoor garden bench with armrests and slatted seat
